---
title: "New 15 Strains"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
date: "2025-11-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
install.packages("data.table")
```
```{r}
install.packages("limma")
```


```{r}
# 1. Load data.table for high-speed loading
library(data.table)
```
```{r}


# 2. Load the matrix (Fastest way on the cluster)
print("Reading file...")
dt <- fread("/cluster/tufts/hussainlab/tkwong01/VMRC/MTMG_outs/VIRGO2_results/Merged_dir/1.MTMG_Merged.txt")

# 3. Create a dummy metadata frame (REPLACE THIS with your actual metadata)
# Your samples are the columns (excluding the first column which is Gene ID)
sample_names <- colnames(dt)[-1] 
metadata <- data.frame(
    SampleID = sample_names,
    Group = rep(c("A", "B"), length.out = length(sample_names)) # Example: Treatment/Control
)

# 4. Prepare for Matrix-wise Regression
# We use limma because a standard 'lm' loop for 1.1 million rows is too slow.
# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("limma")
library(limma)

# Convert to a numeric matrix
gene_ids <- dt[[1]]
mat <- as.matrix(dt[, -1, with=FALSE])
rownames(mat) <- gene_ids

# --- 5. Define the Model with a specific gene as a predictor ---

# Identify the row for your "Predictor Gene" (e.g., 'Gene_Y')
predictor_gene_name <- "Gene_Y" # Replace with your actual Gene ID

# Extract that specific row (it's currently in your 'mat' matrix)
# We transpose it so it becomes a vertical vector of 1,000 values
gene_y_abundance <- as.numeric(mat[predictor_gene_name, ])

# Now, instead of 'Group', we use 'gene_y_abundance' in the model
# This regresses all 1.1 million rows against the values of Gene Y
design <- model.matrix(~ gene_y_abundance)

# --- 6. Run the Linear Models (Same as before) ---
print(paste("Running 1.1 million regressions against", predictor_gene_name))
fit <- lmFit(mat, design)
fit <- eBayes(fit)

# --- 7. Extract Results ---
# coef=2 refers to the effect size (slope) of Gene Y's abundance
results <- topTable(fit, coef=2, number=Inf, sort.by="P")
```
